description: git worktree add refuses to check out a branch that's already checked out elsewhere. For project burrows, the host clone always has main (or some branch) checked out, so a worktree on the same branch fails with 'already used by worktree at ...'.
resolution: materializeProjectWorkspace defaults to createBranch=true with baseBranch='main', carving a per-burrow branch (e.g. burrow/<bur-id>). Pass createBranch=false only when the caller pre-created an isolated branch.

description: git worktree remove rejects worktrees containing modified or untracked files. Burrow's workspace always carries .gitconfig.burrow (and whatever the agent created), so non-forced removal always errors out.
resolution: removeMaterializedWorkspace defaults force=true. Callers can opt out by passing force:false explicitly.

content: Per-burrow git identity goes into <workspace>/.gitconfig.burrow (a file inside the burrow). The agent runtime sets GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL to that path so identity applies inside the burrow without ever mutating the user's host clone (no 'git config --worktree', no flipping extensions.worktreeConfig on the shared .git/).

name: workspace-materialize-with-clone-fallback
description: materializeProjectWorkspace probes for a host clone via discoverHostClone(projectRoot). If found → git worktree add against it. If not → falls back to cloneRepo(originUrl). Throws WorkspaceMaterializationError when neither is available. Lets 'burrow up' work both inside and outside an existing checkout.
